SEARCH
モジュール org.commonutils

パッケージ org.commonutils.id

package org.commonutils.id
Identifier generators with a shared IdGenerator contract.

Main entry points

  • IdGenerator — common generate() procedure
  • UuidV4IdGenerator — RFC 4122 version-4 UUIDs
  • UuidV7 — RFC 9562 version-7 UUIDs (Instant, Clock, batch and range helpers)
  • UuidV7IdGenerator — IdGenerator for RFC 9562 version-7 UUIDs (fixed Instant or per-call Clock)
  • NanoIdGenerator — short URL-safe random strings (default alphabet / configurable), unlike fixed-width binary UUIDs
  • Ulid — ULID strings (48-bit Unix milliseconds + 80-bit randomness, Crockford Base32; fromInstant / fromClock and Ulid.encode(long, byte[]) for tests)
  • UlidIdGenerator — IdGenerator for ULIDs (fixed Instant or per-call Clock); nonCryptographic uses SplittableRandom-backed entropy (see class Javadoc)
  • MutableNanoIdGenerator — Nano ID-style strings whose alphabet and length can change after construction (configure / setAlphabet / setSize); prefer NanoIdGenerator when configuration is fixed

Nano ID generators

Defaults mirror the reference Nano ID alphabet and length. Static nonCryptographic… factories on NanoIdGenerator and MutableNanoIdGenerator opt into a fast SplittableRandom-backed RandomGenerator; see each class's API note in its class documentation for security guidance.

Ecosystem-specific encodings (alternate alphabets imposed by plugins or external tools without a stable specification here) are intentionally not implemented in this library until there is an agreed format and compatibility policy.

ULID vs UUID v7 vs Nano ID

ULID (Ulid / UlidIdGenerator) encodes the same 48-bit Unix millisecond idea as UuidV7 into a fixed-length, lexicographically sortable Crockford Base32 string, which is convenient for human-readable keys and many text-first storage formats.

UUID version 7 is a standardized binary UUID (UUID) in RFC 9562; use UuidV7 / UuidV7IdGenerator when you need UUID types and tooling.

Nano ID (NanoIdGenerator) produces shorter, URL-safe strings with a configurable alphabet and length; unlike ULID it does not embed a timestamp in a standardized layout.

Choosing UUID version

Version 4 (RFC 4122) embeds no timestamp: identifiers are random. Use UuidV4IdGenerator or UUID.randomUUID() when you need opaque, unpredictable IDs without time semantics.

Version 7 (RFC 9562) carries Unix milliseconds in the most significant bits for rough time order and sorting. Use UuidV7 or UuidV7IdGenerator when you need that encoding or test-friendly control via Clock; version 4 deliberately does not accept Clock or date-time parameters.

  • クラス
    説明
    IdGenerator<T>
    Generates identifiers of type T.
    MutableNanoIdGenerator
    IdGenerator for mutable Nano ID-style strings: alphabet and length can be changed after construction while sharing one RandomGenerator.
    NanoIdGenerator
    IdGenerator for Nano ID-style strings: a random, URL-safe identifier using a fixed alphabet and length, with bias-free index selection (reject masked values outside the alphabet range).
    Ulid
    Static factory for ULIDs: 48-bit Unix millisecond timestamp (Instant.toEpochMilli() semantics) in the most significant bits, 80 bits of randomness, encoded as 26 uppercase Crockford Base32 characters.
    UlidIdGenerator
    IdGenerator for ULID strings (Crockford Base32 encoding of 48-bit Unix epoch milliseconds and 80 bits of randomness).
    UuidV4IdGenerator
    IdGenerator for RFC 4122 version-4 (random) UUIDs.
    UuidV7
    Static factory for RFC 9562 UUID version 7 (Unix millisecond timestamp in the most significant bits, random in the remainder).
    UuidV7IdGenerator
    IdGenerator for RFC 9562 UUID version 7.
